concept: Touch / To Feel with the Skin
description: The act of perception through physical contact with the skin. It emphasizes a direct, unmediated, and intimate way of knowing the world.
rationale: The word 'pua' is sonically direct and embodied. The syllable 'pu-' uses a gentle plosive 'p' to signify a specific point of contact, while the 'u' grounds the sensation in the body (Pre-industrial). The vowel hiatus 'u-a' represents an internal sensation ('u') becoming a conscious, present-moment awareness ('a') (Buddhist).
